content.facebook:
One technique that separates good swimmers from great ones? The underwater phase off every wall.

Your dolphin kicks after each turn are where you can genuinely gain on the field. The water is smoother down there, resistance is lower, and a tight streamline with powerful, rhythmic kicks keeps your momentum alive longer than any stroke cycle above the surface.

Here's a drill to sharpen it this week: after each push-off, count your dolphin kicks before you surface. Start with 3, build to 5, then 7. Focus on keeping your core locked, your kick tight from the hip, and your hands stacked. If your hips are snaking or your hands are drifting apart, you're bleeding speed.

Elite swimmers aren't just fast in the open water. They're relentless about the details that add up to podiums.

When you're ready to pair that technique with gear that won't hold you back, our Italian-crafted competition suits are built for exactly this. Minimal drag. Maximum feel. Made for swimmers who take the sport seriously.

Train smart. Race faster. 🏊‍♂️
